narrative.headline: 2019 Mazda CX-5: engine and electrical lead the complaints
narrative.summary: Of 257 owner complaints filed with NHTSA, the engine is by far the most reported area at 124 reports (48%), with one linked to a fire. Electrical problems come next at 45 reports (18%), tied to 2 crashes, 1 fire, and 1 injury. Power train rounds out the top three at 29 reports (11%), with 2 crashes and 2 injuries. Less common but worth knowing about: false forward-collision braking and fuel-system issues.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The single biggest concern: 124 reports (48% of all complaints), including 1 fire. Watch for rough running or stalling-like behavior while driving.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 45 reports (18%), with 2 crashes, 1 fire, and 1 injury. Owners describe the dash and center screen going black mid-drive.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Third most reported: 29 reports (11%), linked to 2 crashes and 2 injuries. A safety-relevant area worth checking on a test drive.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 16 reports (6%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners report the system braking hard when it misjudges traffic ahead.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 15 reports (6%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Complaints center on the fuel system, such as a late low-fuel warning.
